title: WorldSkills Northern Mindanao 2026 Set to Elevate TVET Excellence Through TESDA-MSU-IIT Partnership
description: Following the recent invitation of TESDA Secretary Kiko B. Benitez, duly represented by Deputy Director General for TESD Operations Vidal D. Villanueva III, to the Mindanao State University – Iligan Institute of Technology (MSU-IIT) TVET Forum 2026 in Iligan City, a significant pre-forum discussion set the tone for elevating Technical-Vocational Education and Training (TVET) onto a broader and more competitive stage. Central to the discussion was the agreement to conduct a Regional Skills Competition, to be branded as WorldSkills Northern Mindanao 2026—a strategic move envisioned to showcase excellence, strengthen industry relevance, and professionalize TVET within an academic and innovation-driven environment. Present during the discussion were Chancellor Prof. Alizedney M. Ditucalan, JD, LLM, along with university officials, TESDA Regional Director Rafael Y. Abrogar II, and Provincial Director Norayah A. Acas, JD, all of whom expressed strong support for the initiative. As emphasized by Chancellor Ditucalan, 'With the recent achievements of our mechatronics team in WorldSkills ASEAN Manila and WorldSkills Taipei 2025, this presents a compelling opportunity to further elevate and professionalize TVET—positioning MSU-IIT as a dynamic platform where skills excellence is developed, demonstrated, and celebrated'. TESDA Region X looks forward to this honorable endeavor and meaningful collaboration with MSU-IIT, united in championing TVET as a driver of innovation, competitiveness, and inclusive growth. Through this partnership, the region aims to inspire a new generation of skilled professionals and reinforce the vital role of TVET in national development.

title: COBSAT Marks Earth Month 2026 with Clean-Up Drive and Environmental Awareness Symposium
description: Cagayan de Oro (Bugo) School of Arts and Trades actively joined the Earth Month 2026 celebration with the theme 'Our Power, Our Planet: Driving Sustainability through Green Skills.' As part of the celebration, COBSAT conducted a Clean-Up Drive titled “Pulot Basura” on April 13, 2026, starting at 3:30 PM. Short Course trainees cleaned inside the campus while Diploma Program trainees extended their clean-up activity outside the school premises. Another highlight was the Earth Month Symposium on Environmental Awareness and Solid Waste Management held on April 23, 2026, from 9:00 AM to 12:00 NN. The activity was attended by all trainers and trainees. The program was formally opened with a message from Dr. Miraflor B. Emata, VSA. This was followed by a talk from Engr. Czarine N. Maderal of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources Environmental Management Bureau Region X, who served as the resource speaker. After the lecture, an open forum was conducted where participants actively engaged in discussions on environmental protection and sustainable practices.

title: MSU-IIT Honors 70 Mechatronics NC III Graduates in Milestone Completion Ceremony
description: A truly proud and heartfelt graduation moment unfolded as 70 Mechatronics NC III graduates of MSU-IIT finally reached the end of their training journey and were recognized at the Conference Room, 2nd Floor Prism Building, MSU-IIT on April 23, 2026. Coming right after the successful opening of the TVET Forum facilitated by MSU-IIT, the occasion was made even more meaningful with the presence of DDG Vidal Villanueva III, Regional Director Rafael Y. Abrogar II, Provincial Director Norayah A. Acas, and Chancellor Alizedney M. Ditucalan, who all witnessed not just a formal program, but the real stories of hard work, sleepless nights, hands-on learning, and perseverance behind every graduate’s success. The celebration also reflected the strong commitment of TESDA and MSU-IIT in nurturing competent and industry-ready graduates, further inspired by the Mechatronics team’s previous wins in WorldSkills competition, reminding everyone that the skills built today are not just for graduation, but for greater opportunities and global possibilities ahead.

title: Advancing Future-Ready Skills: TESDA and MSU-IIT Unite to Strengthen TVET Excellence
description: The TVET Forum 2026 facilitated by MSU IIT held at the University Library Theater, MSU IIT last April 23, 2026 stood as a strong testament to the shared commitment of TESDA and MSU-IIT in advancing future-ready, skills-based education. The forum led by MSU IIT Chancellor Alizedney M. Ditucalan became a meaningful platform for strengthening collaboration, promoting innovation, and aligning training programs with industry demands to ensure workforce readiness. From the perspective of TESDA, the event reaffirmed its continued mandate to elevate technical education and skills development through strong partnerships with institutions like MSU-IIT, ensuring that learners are equipped not only with competencies but with confidence to thrive in real-world and global environments. The forum was well-attended by key TESDA officials led by Deputy Director General Vidal Villanueva III, Regional Director Rafael Y. Abrogar II, Provincial Director Norayah A. Acas, along with Director John Kelvin R. Briones and Chief TESD Specialist Jacqueline Jamillah J. Ali, whose presence highlighted TESDA’s strong and hands-on support for TVET implementation at both regional and institutional levels. Through this collaboration with MSU-IIT, TESDA continues its commitment to making technical-vocational education more accessible, relevant, and responsive, ultimately empowering learners with real skills and opportunities, and helping shape a capable, confident, and future-ready workforce.

title: RTC-Iligan Empowers Senior Citizens with Cookies and Tart Making Skills Training
description: The Regional Training Center-Iligan conducted a skills training on Cookies and Tart Making for senior citizens of Brgy. Del Carmen, Iligan City, led by trainer Ms. Mary Heinz B. Venus from April 15-16, 2026. The participants learned essential baking techniques, including dough preparation, proper mixing, baking procedures, and presentation of cookies and tarts—equipping them with practical skills for livelihood and personal use. All participants successfully completed the training and gained additional knowledge to support active aging and community engagement.

title: TESDA, DOT, and PCG Unite to Standardize Lifeguard Certification in Northern Mindanao
description: On April 21, 2026, a coordination meeting was held at the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) District Northern Mindanao Headquarters in Laguindingan, Misamis Oriental, involving representatives from the Department of Tourism (DOT), the PCG, and TESDA Misamis Oriental. The primary goal of this collaboration is to professionalize lifeguard services across the region by ensuring all active and prospective lifeguards are properly trained and certified under the Lifeguard Services NC II qualification. Key attendees included CAPT Christie C. Cabuyaban of the PCG, Irene L. Bruñola from the TESDA Provincial Training Center – Misamis Oriental, and representatives from the DOT and the TESDA Regional Office. During the discussion, the agencies emphasized that standardized certification is critical for enhancing public safety, particularly in coastal areas and tourist destinations. To support this initiative, TESDA committed to providing technical assistance for program registration and conducting a Training of Trainers (TOT) to capacitate qualified personnel as future instructors and assessors. This partnership aims to align national training regulations with local tourism needs, ensuring a high standard of competency for lifeguard services in Northern Mindanao.

title: KNAS Conducts Organic Farming Training to Support Community Rehabilitation and Livelihood Skills
description: On April 7, 2026, the Kinoguitan National Agricultural School (KNAS) conducted a Community-Based Training on Preparing Organic Concoction (FPJ) at the Municipal Gymnasium in Kinoguitan, Misamis Oriental. Facilitated by Ms. Helen G. Pelaez, the activity was part of the Community-Based Drug Rehabilitation Program (CBDRP) and was attended by six participants, including three program beneficiaries and three facilitators. The training was designed to support the rehabilitation and social reintegration of participants by providing them with practical skills in sustainable, eco-friendly agriculture and alternative livelihood opportunities. The training session featured an introduction to organic agriculture followed by a step-by-step demonstration of preparing organic concoctions. Participants actively engaged in hands-on activities, focusing on correct measurement, mixing procedures, and proper storage techniques under the supervision of the resource person.

title: 270 Students Showcase Skills in Misamis Oriental During SHS National Assessment Week 2026
description: The Senior High School National Assessment Week held from March 23-28, 2026 successfully highlighted the technical proficiency and dedication of students across Misamis Oriental. Under the leadership of Provincial Director Marigold Cherie R. Garrido and with the support of Regional Director Rafael Y. Abrograr II, this initiative provided a vital platform for 270 candidates to demonstrate their skills in various qualifications, including SMAW NC II, EIM NC II, Cookery NC II, and Bread and Pastry Production NC II. This undertaking served as a valuable assessment of student performance and curriculum effectiveness, ensuring that our learners are well-prepared for their future professional endeavors. The activity spanned multiple assessment centers fostering a collaborative environment for public and private schools and Alternative Learning Systems (ALS). Despite the challenges faced throughout the week, the program met its core objectives of motivating students and contributing to essential educational planning for the region. We remain committed to expanding these opportunities and strengthening the certification support program to further empower the next generation of skilled Filipino workers.

title: RTC-Iligan Provides Crop Care Training to Support Rehabilitation and Livelihood for PWUDs
description: On April 14, 2026 - the Regional Training Center-Iligan, spearheaded by Center Administrator Sittie Aliah A. Limbao, SCL, JD, together with trainer Ms. Marry Grace M. Magsayo, conducted a three-day community-based training on Integrated Crop Care Practices for 14 People Who Use Drugs (PWUDs) at the Balay Silangan Reformatory Center in Iligan City. The activity equipped the participants with basic knowledge and hands-on skills in proper crop care, enabling them to develop practical competencies for reintegration and sustainable livelihood. The participants expressed their gratitude to TESDA for providing opportunities that support personal growth and positive transformation.
